title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Cardinality Forensics
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Set Operations
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-set-operations-b7px3b" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; subquery, CTE, recursion, or set-operation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Set Operations into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Set Operations, keep Set Operations connected to executable relational evidence.

UNION

ORDER BY team_id;

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.
